title: WitEngine controller manifest
description: The controller.json file bundled at the root of every WitEngine controller module. Emitted by Build/OutWit.Controller.Manifest.targets from per-csproj metadata at build time; read at runtime by OutWit.Engine.Assets (for dataAssets resolution) and OutWit.Cloud.Data (for the rest of the metadata). Extra properties not described here pass through both readers untouched.

name.description: PascalCase controller identifier. Sourced from <ControllerName> in the csproj. Must match the [WitPluginManifest] name attribute on the controller's host class and is what consumers reference via <ControllerDependency Include="<name>" />.

assemblyName.description: Physical .NET assembly filename (without extension) at the root of the module. Sourced from <AssemblyName> (which defaults to the csproj filename, e.g. OutWit.Controller.Render). Used by the loader to discover the entry assembly inside <name>.module/.

version.description: Semantic version string. Sourced from <Version> in the csproj. Used by the dependency resolver — consumers declare a minimum version, the resolver picks the highest matching one. Pre-release / build-metadata suffixes (1.2.0-rc.1, 1.2.0+sha.abcd) are allowed.

description.description: One-line summary shown in the controller browser / package listings. Sourced from <Description>.

authors.description: Author names or organizations. Sourced from <Authors> in the csproj (semicolon-separated); empty array when <Authors> is unset.

copyright.description: Copyright notice. Sourced from <Copyright> in the csproj. Omitted from the manifest when the property is unset.

license.description: SPDX license expression. Sourced from <PackageLicenseExpression>. The shared Build/OutWit.Controller.props injects MIT by default for first-party controllers.

tags.description: Free-form search tags. Sourced from <PackageTags> (semicolon-separated). Conventionally includes "witengine", "witcloud", "controller" plus per-controller topical tags.

features.description: Stable capability identifiers a consumer can match against ("render-frame", "sparse-matrix", "tiled-still", ...). Sourced from <ControllerFeatures>. Treated as a public API surface — once added, removing one is a breaking change.

useCases.description: Human-readable use-case summaries for the controller browser. Sourced from <ControllerUseCases>. No stability contract — purely descriptive.

runtimeTargets.description: Platforms the controller declares support for. Sourced from <ControllerRuntimeTarget> items. Empty array means platform-agnostic (a pure-.NET controller with no native assets). RIDs listed here gate which <ControllerDataAsset> entries get resolved on the current host.

dependencies.description: Controller-to-controller version constraints. Keys are controller `name`s, values are minimum versions. Sourced from <ControllerDependency Include="..." MinimumVersion="..." /> items. Resolver picks the highest installed version that is >= the constraint.

dataAssets.description:
External blobs (native runtimes, datasets, reference scenes) fetched at consumer-build time by OutWit.Engine.Assets.ResolveControllerAssetsTask. Sourced from <ControllerDataAsset> items. Empty for Tier-1 controllers; non-empty for Tier-2 (Render, Matrices).

When non-empty: the consumer-side build/<id>.targets imports this manifest from content/module/controller.json, fetches each entry that matches the host RID (or runtimeIdentifier == "any"), verifies SHA256, and extracts into the module's <extractTo> path.

rid.description: .NET runtime identifier — `win-x64`, `linux-x64`, `osx-arm64`, etc. Matches the values used in <ControllerDataAsset RuntimeIdentifier="..." />.

id.description: Stable identifier within the manifest, used for per-asset diagnostics and to disambiguate multi-platform variants (e.g. `blender-win-x64` vs `blender-linux-x64`).

runtimeIdentifier.description: Which RID this asset is needed on. `any` (default) means every host fetches it; a specific RID like `win-x64` gates the asset to that platform only. Must either be `any` or appear in the `runtimeTargets[*].rid` list.

uri.description:
Where to fetch the asset. Schemes understood by the built-in resolvers:
- `gh-release://<owner>/<repo>/<tag>/<file>` — GitHub Release artefact.
- `https://...` — arbitrary HTTPS URL.
- `file://...` — local path (typically for inline-packed Path-B contributor zips).

Third parties can plug additional resolvers via OutWit.Engine.Assets.

sha256.description: Hex SHA-256 digest of the asset bytes. Verified after download; mismatch aborts the fetch. The outwit-assets-pack author tool computes and writes this; manual edits should be 64 lowercase hex chars.

size.description: Expected size of the asset in bytes. Informational — drives progress reporting and pre-flight disk-space estimation. The author tool fills this in alongside the SHA.

extractTo.description: Relative path inside the controller's `.module/` directory where the asset (or, for archives, the extracted contents) should land. `.` means the module root. Leading slashes / path-traversal segments are rejected by the resolver.

required.description: When true (default), the controller cannot run without this asset and the resolver fails loudly on fetch errors. When false, the asset is best-effort — used for optional benchmark fixtures or platform variants the current host doesn't strictly need.
